The Boys in the Boat (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $52,641,306Details
International Box Office $2,880,268
Worldwide Box Office $55,521,574
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $140,501
Total $140,501
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A film about the 1936 University of Washington rowing team that competed for gold at the Summer Olympics in Berlin. This inspirational true story follows a group of underdogs at the height of the Great Depression as they are thrust into the spotlight and take on elite rivals from around the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,400,548 (16.0% of total gross)
Legs:6.27 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:94.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,557 opening theaters/2,687 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $56,453,437

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2023 (Wide) by Amazon MGM Studios
International Releases: January 4th, 2024 (Wide) (Australia)
January 4th, 2024 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 12th, 2024 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: January 16th, 2024 by MGM Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for language and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2767 (cert #54238), 1/25/2023)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Geographical setting: Washington, United States
Plot point: Miscellaneous Racing, On a Boat
Social setting: Olympics
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Time period setting: 1930s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Spyglass Media Group, Smokehouse Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A film about the 1936 University of Washington rowing team that competed for gold at the Summer Olympics in Berlin. This inspirational true story follows a group of underdogs at the height of the Great Depression as they are thrust into the spotlight and take on elite rivals from around the world.
